channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: How dangerous are 3D printers? Maybe enough for a background check
item.description: As 3D-printed gun violence abounds, some lawmakers are looking to cut the problem at the root. The New York state senate is currently evaluating a bill that would dramatically change the 3D printing market.
item.dc:creator: Henry Chandonnet, Fast Company

item.title: New Nvidia RTX 5080 benchmark rumor has got me worried that the GPU won't be a major upgrade to the RTX 4080
item.description: Claimed Nvidia RTX 5080 graphics benchmarks suggest the GPU won’t quite match the RTX 4090 (unlike the RTX 4080 which easily outgunned the 3090).
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Trump AI project presents Musk with early test of loyalty
item.description: Elon Musk’s criticism of an artificial intelligence (AI) infrastructure project backed by President Trump is presenting an early test of how the SpaceX CEO will balance his personal conflicts in the technology space with the interests of the president.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: UK CMA opens investigations into Apple and Google mobile ecosystems
item.description: Apple and Google could end up having to make apps and app content “easier” to obtain from elsewhere, and offer fairer terms to app developers hosting their content on its official stores.
item.dc:creator: luke.hughes@futurenet.com (Luke Hughes), TechRadar

item.title: El 29% de los CDO no ven futuro en el puesto
item.description: Casi un tercio de los líderes de TI que ocupan el puesto de director de datos y funciones similares en empresas de la lista Fortune 1000 no creen en el futuro a largo plazo del puesto de CDO.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: First image for Supergirl: Woman of Tomorrow shows Milly Alcock's Kara Zor-El in a place that'll be very familiar to DC comic book fans
item.description: James Gunn has revealed the first image for Supergirl: Woman of Tomorrow as filming gets underway on the DCU Chapter One movie.
item.dc:creator: tom.power@futurenet.com (Tom Power), TechRadar

item.title: AMD’s unpatched chip microcode glitch may require extreme measures by CISOs
item.description: Due to a partner disclosure, AMD has found itself in the unenviable position of having to confirm the existence of a major cybersecurity problem in its chip microcode before it could post a fix. But that headache is mild compared to those of their customers’ CISOs.
item.dc:creator: csoonline

item.title: TikTok France is being sued by 7 families. Here’s why
item.description: In the moment when her world shattered three years ago, Stephanie Mistre found her 15-year-old daughter, Marie, lifeless in the bedroom where she died by suicide.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Warren sends Musk 30 ideas for DOGE to cut government spending
item.description: Sen. Elizabeth Warren (D-Mass.) sent tech billionaire Elon Musk 30 ideas for how the newly formed Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) could cut government spending.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Trump signs executive order establishing crypto working group
item.description: President Trump signed an executive order Thursday establishing a working group on digital assets led by David Sacks, the White House czar for artificial intelligence (AI) and cryptocurrency.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Setting Digital Boundaries: Navigating Social Media Etiquette
item.description: Learn how to set digital boundaries, deal with social media etiquette dilemmas, and maintain a healthy online presence.
item.dc:creator: Steffi Cao, Fast Company

item.title: Cyber Command’s order creates ‘inflection point’ for DoDIN
item.description: Lt. Gen. Paul Stanton, commander of JFHQ-DoDIN, said commanders can better manage risks and focus on defending the applications and data that matters the most. The post Cyber Command’s order creates ‘inflection point’ for DoDIN first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: The OpenJDK’s plans for Java in 2025
item.description: Java enhancement plans for 2025 range from improving performance in the foreign function and memory (FFM) API, to working on ahead-of-time (AOT) code compilation, to finalizing the structured concurrency API, according to a recently published presentation from the OpenJDK community.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Spain's leader wants the EU to ‘make social media great again.’ Here's how
item.description: DAVOS, Switzerland (AP) — Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez is launching a crusade against what he sees as the downsides of social media.
item.dc:creator: JOSEPH WILSON and JAMEY KEATEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Tips on overcoming the loss of cherished, personal belongings in disasters
item.description: Losing important sentimental belongings — those items that represented who you are — can be traumatic for those who go through disasters that destroy homes.
item.dc:creator: JULIA RUBIN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Pakistan's parliament passes bill with sweeping controls on social media
item.description: ISLAMABAD (AP) — Pakistan’s lower house of parliament on Thursday passed a controversial bill that will give the government sweeping controls on social media, including sending users to prison for spreading disinformation.
item.dc:creator: MUNIR AHMED, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: This flying motorcycle can take you from traffic to sky in minutes
item.description: The Skyrider X1 from Rictor claims to be the "first amphibious flying passenger motorcycle," and it combines land and air travel in one sleek design.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump pardons founder of Silk Road website
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump announced Tuesday that he had pardoned Ross Ulbricht, the founder of Silk Road, an underground website for selling drugs.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump orders government not to infringe on Americans’ speech, calls for censorship investigation
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — President Donald Trump on Monday ordered that no federal officer, employee or agent may unconstitutionally abridge the free speech of any American citizen, an early step toward his campaign promise to dismantle what he called government “censorship” of U.S. citizens.
item.dc:creator: ALI SWENSON, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: China gains ground
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: That Apple ID disabled message? It's a dangerous scam
item.description: Kurt the CyberGuy breaks down why those emails that warn that "Your Apple ID has been disabled" is most likely a scam. Here's how to protect yourself.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: TikTok refugees are pouring to Xiaohongshu. Here's what you need to know about the RedNote app
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — As the fate of TikTok hangs in the balance, U.S. TikTok users are flocking to the Chinese social media app Xiaohongshu, also called RedNote – making it the top downloaded app in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: FU TING and DAVID COHEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Who could buy TikTok? Only a few parties are serious about offering
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The supremely popular TikTok could be banned on Jan. 19 under a federal law that forces the video sharing platform to divest itself from its China-based parent company, ByteDance, or shut down its U.S. operations.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4
